description:

THEN
  · BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag := 1
  · Trigger SOH re-calibration request
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit, resulting in BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag being set to 1 and an SOH re-calibration request being triggered.
Test the boundary condition where cumulative charge throughput is very close to but does not exceed the design cycle limit, ensuring that BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag remains unset.
Test a scenario where there is an error in setting BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag or triggering the SOH re-calibration request when it should occur due to exceeding the design cycle limit.
Verify that after the cumulative charge throughput exceeds the design cycle limit, the system transitions correctly from a normal state to a fault state and triggers the appropriate response.

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior when BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1. Verify that all main contactors are opened within 100 ms and BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_HVILFault_Flag changes from 0 to 1 at exactly 100 ms. Ensure that the system responds correctly without any delay or glitch.
Simulate a scenario where BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1 but does not change back within 100 ms. Verify that the system still opens all main contactors and transitions to SafeState as expected.
Test the state transition from any other state to SafeState when BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1. Ensure that the system handles this transition smoothly without any issues.
Verify the observability of the BMS_HVILFault_Flag signal by monitoring its value before and after triggering the fault condition. Ensure that the flag accurately reflects the fault state.

description:
IF  measured isolation resistance < r_Insulation_MinThreshold
THEN
  · BMS_InsulationFault_Flag := 1
  · Open both main contactors
supplementary_info:
Function: Insulation & HVIL
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring
Context: Isolation resistance monitoring is the primary safety mechanism for detecting insulation degradation between the HV power system and vehicle chassis. The measurement is performed continuously and a fault triggers immediate contactor opening. If the fault persists, it is latched and requires a diagnostic clear to recover:
- BMS_InsulationFault_Flag = 1: isolation resistance < r_Insulation_MinThreshold, contactors open
- BMS_InsulationFault_Latch = 1: fault persisted >= 1 s, latched until diagnostic clear

analysis.direction:
triggering: BMS_InsulationFault_Flag := 1 when measured isolation resistance < r_Insulation_MinThreshold
clearing: BMS_InsulationFault_Latch = 0 after diagnostic clear
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test the boundary condition where measured isolation resistance equals r_Insulation_MinThreshold to ensure no false trigger.
Test that both main contactors are opened when BMS_InsulationFault_Flag is triggered due to low isolation resistance.
Verify the latching behavior of BMS_InsulationFault_Latch after 1 second if fault persists without a diagnostic clear.
Ensure that the system can detect and report an insulation fault correctly through BMS_InsulationFault_Flag and BMS_InsulationFault_Latch signals.

description:
IF  any cell temperature <= low temperature limit
THEN
  · BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1
  · BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection
Context: Low temperature protection prevents charging when cells are too cold to accept current without damage, and manages the battery heater to enable charging under cold conditions. The heater is de-energized on overtemperature or when no longer needed:
- BMS_CellUT_Flag = 1: any cell <= low temperature limit (charging prohibited, BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 0)
- BMS_HeaterOT_Flag = 1: heater temperature exceeds safe limit
- Heater activation threshold: cell <= -10 C during charge session

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1 when any cell temperature <= low temperature limit; clearing: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where a single cell temperature is below the low temperature limit, causing BMS_CellUT_Flag to be set and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag to be cleared.
Test the boundary condition where exactly one cell's temperature equals the low temperature limit, ensuring that BMS_CellUT_Flag is still triggered.
Test a scenario where multiple cells are below the low temperature limit to ensure all BMS_CellUT_Flag instances trigger correctly and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag remains cleared.
Test transitioning from normal operation to cold conditions, then back to normal after heaters activate to manage under-temperature protection effectively.
Verify that the system responds appropriately when a cell temperature is above and below the low temperature limit threshold without any explicit thresholds provided.

description:
IF  HV leakage current > safety threshold
THEN
  · BMS_HVLeakage_Flag := 1
  · Trigger audible warning via HMI
supplementary_info:
Function: Insulation & HVIL
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection
Context: HV leakage monitoring provides an additional layer of protection beyond isolation resistance by detecting active current leakage through insulation breakdown. An internal fault in the monitoring device itself is also detected and reported. If a redundant measurement path is available, monitoring continues:
- BMS_HVLeakage_Flag = 1: leakage current > safety threshold, HMI warning triggered
- BMS_InsulationSensorFault_Flag = 1: monitoring device internal fault

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_HVLeakage_Flag := 1; Trigger audible warning via HMI
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where HV leakage current exceeds the safety threshold, causing BMS_HVLeakage_Flag to be set and an audible warning via HMI to be triggered.
Test the boundary condition where the HV leakage current is exactly at the safety threshold but not exceeding it, ensuring that BMS_HVLeakage_Flag remains cleared.
Test the internal fault scenario where BMS_InsulationSensorFault_Flag is set while BMS_HVLeakage_Flag is also set to ensure proper handling of redundant monitoring paths.
Test the state transition from normal operation to leakage detection and warning, including the behavior when the leakage current returns below the threshold.

description:
IF  CAN bus-off or excessive error passive detected
THEN
  · BMS_CANFault_Flag := 1
  · Enter limp-home mode (discharge power <= 30 %)
  · BMS_SafeState_Entry := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Communication & Diagnostics
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault
Context: CAN communication faults compromise the BMS ability to coordinate with VCU and other vehicle systems. Upon fault detection, the BMS enters a limp-home mode with reduced functionality while maintaining essential safety monitoring. Normal operation resumes when communication is restored and the fault condition clears:
- BMS_CANFault_Flag = 1: CAN bus-off or excessive error passive detected
- BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1: request safe state entry
- Limp-home mode: discharge limited to 30 %, basic safety functions maintained

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CANFault_Flag := 1, BMS_SafeState_Entry := 1; clearing: none specified
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

description:
Upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault:
  · Open both main contactors
  · Discharge DC link capacitance
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Deactivating
  · THEN Standby or SafeState as appropriate
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation > 13.3 HV Deactivation and Sleep
Context: HV deactivation is triggered by normal shutdown requests or fault conditions from any protection function (sections 1-12). After contactors open and the DC link discharges, the system may enter low-power sleep mode to conserve quiescent current while maintaining wake-up capability:
- Deactivation: open contactors, discharge DC link, transition through HV_Deactivating to Standby or SafeState
- Sleep entry: Standby with no CAN activity for configurable timeout

description:
Upon BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, the BMS shall:
  · Broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity
  · Reduce charge current target to 50 % of nominal
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating
Context: When a confirmed overvoltage fault is present, the BMS initiates a controlled derating phase before resorting to contactor-level actions. Warning signals are broadcast on CAN for VCU coordination:
- BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1: overvoltage confirmed (BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1)
  Transmission: CAN ID 0x180, periodicity 100 ms
- BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1: charge termination requested
- Charge current target reduced to 50 % of nominal during derating

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 triggers BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 and charge current reduction.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of the BMS when BMS_CellOV_Flag is triggered to 1, ensuring that BMS_CellOV_Warn is broadcasted on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity and charge current target is reduced to 50 % of nominal.

Test the boundary condition where BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 0 to 1, ensuring that BMS_CellOV_Warn is correctly broadcasted on CAN ID 0x180 at the start of the periodicity (i.e., within the first 100 ms).
Test the boundary condition where BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 1 to 0, ensuring that BMS_CellOV_Warn is cleared and charge current target returns to nominal.
Test the state transition of the BMS when a new overvoltage event occurs while already in derating mode, ensuring that the periodic broadcasting of BMS_CellOV_Warn continues without interruption.
Verify the observability of the system by monitoring the CAN bus for correct transmission of BMS_CellOV_Warn and charge current target reduction during an overvoltage event.

description:
On VBAT application, the BMS shall:
  · Initialise all subsystems
  · Perform memory self-check
  · Load calibration parameters from NVM
  · Verify sensor communication
Upon successful initialisation: BMS_SystemState := Standby
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization
Context: The BMS state machine defines the operating modes of the battery system and governs transitions between them. All protection functions (sections 1-12) reference the current system state for context-appropriate response. Initialisation on power-up performs self-checks before entering Standby:
- BMS_SystemState: 0 = Off, 1 = Standby, 2 = PrechargeActive, 3 = HV_Active, 4 = HV_Deactivating, 5 = SafeState, 6 = HV_Failure
- Wake-up sources: CAN activity or dedicated wake-up pin
- Initialisation target: 500 ms from wake to CAN report

analysis.direction: none found
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

description: The BMS shall monitor each cell voltage against the overvoltage threshold. BMS_CellOV_Detect shall be set to 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold.
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation
Context: Overvoltage protection ensures that cell voltages remain within the safe operating range defined by the cell manufacturer. Detection is performed continuously and follows a three-level escalation scheme:
- BMS_CellOV_Detect = 1: cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold (raw detection)
- BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1: detection debounced for >= t_CellOV_Debounce (confirmed fault)
- BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag = 1: voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold (critical, contactor opening) | The following calibration parameters define the overvoltage detection thresholds and timing:
- r_CellOV_Threshold: detection level [4.15 V - 4.35 V]
- t_CellOV_Debounce: confirmation debounce time [50 ms - 500 ms]
- r_CellOV_L3_Threshold: critical overvoltage level [4.25 V - 4.40 V]

analysis.direction:
BMS_CellOV_Detect shall be set to 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold.
BMS_CellOV_Flag and BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag are triggered after the debounce period t_CellOV_Debounce.
missing_critical_info: none

Test the state transition from BMS_CellOV_Detect to BMS_CellOV_Flag during the debounce period.

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_InsulationFault_Flag remains high for exactly 1 second and triggers the latch and other actions.

Test the boundary condition where BMS_InsulationFault_Flag is high for exactly 0.999 seconds, ensuring no action is taken.
Test the state transition from normal operation to fault state and back by toggling BMS_InsulationFault_Flag multiple times within the debounce period.

description:
After both BMS_ChargeOC_Flag = 0 and BMS_DischargeOC_Flag = 0:
  · Power limit shall be maintained for 2 s hold time
  · Power shall ramp back to nominal at max 10 % per second
  · A new overcurrent event during ramp shall reset the hold timer
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a behavior where the system should maintain power limits and ramp back to nominal, with specific timing constraints. The direction is triggering on the hold timer and ramping down.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test that the system maintains power limits for 2 seconds when both BMS_ChargeOC_Flag and BMS_DischargeOC_Flag are cleared.
Verify that the power ramps back to nominal at a rate of no more than 10% per second after the hold time.
Ensure that a new overcurrent event resets the hold timer and the system starts the process again from the beginning.
Check that the system transitions correctly from the hold state to the ramping state when both BMS_ChargeOC_Flag and BMS_DischargeOC_Flag are cleared.
Confirm that the system accurately measures and reports the power levels during the ramp back process.

description:
IF  pack charge current exceeds 150 A
THEN
  · BMS_ChargeOC_Flag := 1
  · Limit charge current to 100 A within 50 ms
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection
Context: Overcurrent protection monitors charge and discharge currents independently, as they imply different hazard profiles. Charge overcurrent primarily risks cell overvoltage (see section 1), while discharge overcurrent may indicate a load-side fault. All thresholds are calibration parameters:
- BMS_ChargeOC_Flag = 1: charge current > 150 A
- BMS_DischargeOC_Flag = 1: discharge current > 200 A
- Recovery ramp: power restored at max 10 % per second after 2 s hold

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: none

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where charge current is exactly 150 A, and BMS_ChargeOC_Flag remains unchanged.
Test that BMS_ChargeOC_Flag is set to 1 when charge current exceeds 150 A.
Verify the system limits charge current to 100 A within 50 ms after BMS_ChargeOC_Flag is triggered.
Confirm that BMS_ChargeOC_Flag returns to 0 when the charge current drops below 150 A and remains stable over time.

description:
A charge session shall be terminated on any of the following conditions:
  · BMS_ChargeComplete_Flag = 1
  · BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 (from section 1 overvoltage)
  · Any active fault requiring contactor open (sections 1-8)
  · Charger disconnected
supplementary_info:
Function: Charge Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a triggering behavior: the charge session is terminated based on certain conditions being met.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test the normal termination of the charge session when the charger is disconnected.
